title: Bug: Fix a link in README: AutoComplete for Search Engines
author: dev-ayush101
body: Under System Design Interview Problems, the link for `AutoComplete for Search Engines` land to "Design Instagram" on algomaster. Request to fix that. Thanks

title: Add Observability Fundamentals section to the repository
author: sudheerxdev
body:
The repository already provides excellent coverage of distributed systems and system design concepts. However, observability-related topics are currently either partially covered or scattered across different sections.

Suggested Improvement

Add a dedicated Observability Fundamentals section containing resources related to:

Metrics
Logging
Monitoring
Distributed Tracing
OpenTelemetry basics
Alerting strategies
Why this would help
Observability is a core part of modern distributed systems and backend engineering.
It helps learners understand how production systems are monitored and debugged.
It improves the repository’s completeness for system design interviews and real-world engineering concepts.
Possible Resources
Prometheus
Grafana
OpenTelemetry
Distributed tracing articles
RED/USE monitoring methodologies

I would be happy to contribute relevant high-quality resources if this enhancement is approved.

title: Add more system design concept articles and learning resources
author: kruthikroshan
body:
## 📚 Description
The repository is a great collection of system design resources, but it can be further improved by adding more concept-focused articles.

## 🚀 Suggested Improvement
Add a dedicated section or expand existing sections with articles covering key system design concepts such as:
- Scalability
- Load Balancing
- Caching strategies
- Database sharding
- CAP theorem
- Microservices architecture

## 🎯 Benefits
- Helps beginners understand core concepts more clearly
- Enhances the repository’s value for interview preparation
- Keeps the content up-to-date and comprehensive

## 💡 Additional Notes
I would like to contribute by adding relevant resources if this enhancement is approved.

title: Practical Resources for System Design and ML Interview Questions (repo+website)
author: Ali-Meh619
body:
Hi everyone and @ashishps1,

I’ve been preparing for system design and machine learning interviews and started organizing my notes into a structured guide.

It covers topics like:

• System design fundamentals (load balancing, caching, distributed systems)
• Scalable architecture patterns
• Machine learning concepts often asked in interviews
• ML system design and deployment basics
• Practical patterns and explanations for common interview questions

The goal is to keep explanations concise and practical so engineers can quickly review key concepts before interviews.

GitHub repo:
[https://github.com/Ali-Meh619/System_Design_ML_Principles](https://github.com/Ali-Meh619/System_Design_ML_Principles)

Website version:
[https://ali-meh619.github.io/System_Design_ML_Principles/site/index.html](https://ali-meh619.github.io/System_Design_ML_Principles/site/index.html)

It is still in progress. If anyone has suggestions for topics I should add or improve, I’d really appreciate the feedback.

Thanks

title: Great repo! Thank you - where did you get these questions from?
author: chris544460
body: Hi there, I really appreciate the effort you put into compiling this repository of system design resources. It's been very helpful for my preparation. I was curious about the origins of the questions you included in the repo. Could you share where you sourced them from? Thanks!

title: Add GitHub Issue Templates
author: ajitzero
body:
Many dummy issues are being created, possibly by people unfamiliar with the GitHub interface. Using GitHub Issue Templates should help reduce these unintentional issues raised.

Reference on how to create issue templates: https://docs.github.com/en/communities/using-templates-to-encourage-useful-issues-and-pull-requests/configuring-issue-templates-for-your-repository

I suggest adding the following custom templates:
- New: Submit a new resource.
- Bug: Fix a broken link or typo.
- Other: Suggest general improvements.

---

_Problematic issues so far:_
- #2 #3 #17 #19 #21 #22 #23 #24 #34 
- #4 appears to be feedback from users, but Issues is not the best place for that. Maybe we can consider enabling GitHub Discussions for this repo.

title: Sliding window log issue
author: dithya512m
body:
As per the your [article](https://blog.algomaster.io/i/146668173/sliding-window-log), the request is added to the queue even if the request isn't allowed. But in the [implementation](https://github.com/ashishps1/awesome-system-design-resources/blob/main/implementations/java/rate_limiting/SlidingWindowLog.java) you check if the queue size is less than capacity before adding.

Your LeakyBucket and Sliding Window Log implementations are almost the same, or am I missing something? The class accepts two parameters and queue polling is the same.

Also, in leakyBucket, queue polling should be done by the server attached to rate limit?
